steps.title: Find the central hypothesis
steps.content: The passage asks why black-footed cats purr while leopards roar. It explains that differences in the hyoid bone and related structures may allow roaring and may prevent purring.

steps.title: Reject narrow summaries
steps.content: Choice A lists one structural difference. Choice B focuses only on why leopards roar. Choice C says researchers are uncertain, but the passage presents a specific hypothesis. The main idea must connect hyoid differences to the purr-roar contrast. The answer is D.
